raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.3,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.5,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.5,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"rationale":{"R1.2":"The pipeline description contains logical sequencing issues — the past-perfect constructions ('we had obtained', 'we had then aggregated') suggest post-hoc rationalization rather than principled design narration; the NMS-then-union logic and K-calibration procedure lack internal consistency checks across stages.","R1.3":"Specific quantitative claims are asserted (0.91 sensitivity at 1 FP/scan on 412 held-out scans, comparison to 'published FROC numbers') without in-text citation anchors to the Setio benchmark or a results table; alignment between these claims and available evidence must be verified.","R1.4":"An explicit novelty claim is staked (contrastive selector vs. synthesis/FROC-optimized ensembles); the actual delta relative to Setio et al. and other multi-model radiology pipelines is asserted but not quantified or bounded, making the novelty framing hard to evaluate.","R1.6":"The prose has pervasive structural problems: sentences are chained through serial 'and' conjunctions spanning four or more clauses, verb tense is inconsistent (past perfect mixed with simple past), and paragraph boundaries do not correspond to distinct pipeline stages, reducing readability significantly.","R1.7":"Multiple specific works are named (Armato et al., Setio et al., LUNA16, NLST) without formal citation markers in the text; reference accuracy, completeness of attribution, and whether the characterization of Setio et al. as 'FROC-optimized' is accurate are all verifiable and relevant to the claims made."},"clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Should this review focus primarily on writing quality and prose (sentence structure, clarity, tense consistency, flow), or primarily on technical and scientific content (methodology soundness, evidence-claim alignment, and novelty claims) — or both equally?"}

rationale.R1.2: The pipeline description contains logical sequencing issues — the past-perfect constructions ('we had obtained', 'we had then aggregated') suggest post-hoc rationalization rather than principled design narration; the NMS-then-union logic and K-calibration procedure lack internal consistency checks across stages.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are asserted (0.91 sensitivity at 1 FP/scan on 412 held-out scans, comparison to 'published FROC numbers') without in-text citation anchors to the Setio benchmark or a results table; alignment between these claims and available evidence must be verified.
rationale.R1.4: An explicit novelty claim is staked (contrastive selector vs. synthesis/FROC-optimized ensembles); the actual delta relative to Setio et al. and other multi-model radiology pipelines is asserted but not quantified or bounded, making the novelty framing hard to evaluate.
rationale.R1.6: The prose has pervasive structural problems: sentences are chained through serial 'and' conjunctions spanning four or more clauses, verb tense is inconsistent (past perfect mixed with simple past), and paragraph boundaries do not correspond to distinct pipeline stages, reducing readability significantly.
rationale.R1.7: Multiple specific works are named (Armato et al., Setio et al., LUNA16, NLST) without formal citation markers in the text; reference accuracy, completeness of attribution, and whether the characterization of Setio et al. as 'FROC-optimized' is accurate are all verifiable and relevant to the claims made.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review focus primarily on writing quality and prose (sentence structure, clarity, tense consistency, flow), or primarily on technical and scientific content (methodology soundness, evidence-claim alignment, and novelty claims) — or both equally?
